text.title: When user-flows.md is required vs skipped
text.summary: The artefact tracks behaviour the user can observe. Pure backend work is verified through API tests, not user-flow specs.

rule.statement: If the feature has any user-facing flow (page navigation, form submission, button click, command invocation), user-flows.md MUST exist before the feature can be promoted to done/.
rule.rationale: A user-facing flow without a documented happy / negative path is a flow nobody is actually testing. The artefact is the only forcing function.

example.note: Feature: "stripe checkout page". user-flows.md documents the happy purchase flow + card-declined negative + 3DS-challenge negative + double-submit prevention. Each flow links to a Playwright spec.

rule.statement: Backend-only features (no rendered surface) MUST NOT have user-flows.md. They verify through API tests in `tests/api/`.
rule.rationale: Forcing user-flows.md on backend work produces empty templates and conditions reviewers to skim past the artefact when it matters.

list.item: Backend API endpoint with no UI consumer in this feature → API tests only.
Background worker / job / cron → unit + integration tests.
Data migration → migration test + reversibility check.

antipattern.reason: There is no user in the flow. The artefact misleads reviewers into looking for a UI that does not exist.
